# Daily Temporal Disruption Report Template

Use this template to convert a real-world event into structured temporal intelligence, educational lessons, satire prompts, and business/media outputs.

## 1. Event
Describe what happened in plain language.

## 2. Theater Name
Give the event a science-fiction, mythic, or systems-level title.

Examples:
- The Hormuz Disruption Gate
- The Eastern Historical Recursion Zone
- The Propaganda Nebula
- The Bureaucratic Delay Fields

## 3. Factual Summary
List the core facts and known actors.

## 4. Historical Echo
Identify what earlier event, pattern, or era this resembles.

## 5. Timeline Failure
Classify the visible failure using `docs/patterns/timeline-failure-taxonomy.md`.

## 6. Alien Interpretation
Explain how the Space Aliens / Time Machines Management Systems layer would classify the event.

## 7. Comedy Shock Response
Write a short satire summary that exposes the most absurd truth in the event.

## 8. Educational Lesson
State what humans, teams, businesses, or AI systems should learn.

## 9. Redesign Proposal
Suggest improved workflows, tools, governance, automation, coordination, or communication patterns.

## 10. Conversion Outputs
Choose which assets to generate.

- article
- ebook section
- dashboard card
- bot prompt
- short video script
- music-film idea
- satire monologue
- knowledge-base entry
- merch slogan

## 11. Tags
Recommended tags:

- fact
- analysis
- satire
- scenario
- redesign
- conversion
- education
- media

## Example Skeleton

```md
# Daily Temporal Disruption Report

## Event
[What happened]

## Theater Name
[Mythic or sci-fi label]

## Factual Summary
- [fact 1]
- [fact 2]
- [fact 3]

## Historical Echo
[What it resembles]

## Timeline Failure
- primary: [category]
- secondary: [category]

## Alien Interpretation
[How the event is classified]

## Comedy Shock Response
[Short satirical truth burst]

## Educational Lesson
[What should be learned]

## Redesign Proposal
[What should be improved]

## Conversion Outputs
- [chosen outputs]
```

## Best Practices

- keep the factual summary sober and clear
- keep satire clearly labeled
- avoid mixing speculation into fact sections
- always include at least one redesign proposal
- prefer repeatable structure over improvisational formatting
